subject: [PATCH 3/3] nfsd: fix UAF in async copy cancel and shutdown

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free (UAF) bug in the NFS daemon's async copy cancel and shutdown functionality. The issue arises when an async copy is canceled or shut down, causing the reaper to move the copy off the async_copies list while another thread tries to access it, leading to a double free of the filecache object. To fix this, the patch modifies find_async_copy() to clear cp_clp and remove the copy from async_copies after incrementing its reference count, ensuring that the reaper can no longer observe the copy. Additionally, nfsd4_stop_copy() is modified to release the list-membership reference held by the reaper before calling cleanup_async_copy().
